title: Data Access with TXT
text: Data Access with TXT The TXT connector reads and writes files that are defined in entity-to-connector assignments in the application assignment file. Reading and Writing Files By default, when you open an entity, the TXT connector creates a new file or overwrites an existing file. You can override this default functionality, so that Uniface appends records to an existing file. Add the /append switch to each filename in an entity-to-connector assignment in the assignment file. For example: my_entity.my_cs = $txt:my_file.ffd/append/uni=UTF16 Copy On Windows and Unix, stream files are created, which means that the record terminator is written to the file in the Unicode encoding of the file. For all Unicode files, the BOM, Byte Order Mark, is written as the first bytes of data, followed by the data of the first record. I/O channels One I/O channel is opened per file. The maximum number of I/O channels is dependent on the operating system. ProcScript Statements The open and close ProcScript statements are supported. The selectdb statement and where clause are not supported. If either of these statements is used, the TXT connector ignores it. Paths Entity-to-connector paths should only be specified for files. For more information, see Configuring the TXT Connector . Logon information is not required. Wildcards are not supported. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
